title: Coleus neochilus
taxonomy: Coleus neochilus is classified in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Coleus, which is now often referred to as Solenostemon. Coleus neochilus is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. The plant is also known as 'false sage' due to its similar appearance to true sage. Further classification details may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Lamiaceae family.
uses: Coleus neochilus is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in South Africa. The leaves of the plant are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis and bronchitis. The plant is also used to treat skin conditions, including eczema and dermatitis. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Coleus neochilus for medicinal purposes.

intro: Coleus neochilus, also known as Sutherlandia frutescens, is a medicinal plant native to southern Africa, traditionally used to support immune function and overall health. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Coleus neochilus are said to include enhanced immune function, improved digestion, and reduced oxidative stress, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Coleus neochilus is often used in teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as in combination with other herbs to create tonics and supplements.
